_comment: Method- and instrument-specific data for the NTC temperature calibration procedure (PRO-CAL-MST-003). Create a sibling file (e.g. calibration_method_humidity.json) for each new measurand and point build_input_json.py at the correct one. Nothing in this file is lab-identity data — that belongs in measurestream_company.json.

sensor_method_template.calibration_method: Internal procedure validated by the quality service laboratory approved by Accredia based on the comparison method in a controlled oven with a reference, Pt100 platinum resistance thermometer read by Fluke 1502A metrology well.
measurement_conditions: Reference standard: Pt100 Platinum Resistance Thermometer read by Fluke 1502A readout (U=0.065 °C, k=2)
Sensor under calibration: DIGITAL thermometer (Measurestream electronics, model 0001-3)
Settling period: 10 min between each acquisition step
traceability_labs: INRIM - Istituto Nazionale di Ricerca Metrologica, Turin
sensor_method_template.traceability: Metrological traceability linked to INRIM national standards via accredited certificates LAT-042-2025-0318 and LAT-042-2025-0319.
sensor_method_template.starting_uncertainties: Reference PT100/Fluke 1502A: U=0.065 °C, k=2. NTC ADC: half-width d=0.30 °C (uniform). Coverage factor k=2, confidence level ~95%.
notes_template: Results refer to the instrument under calibration under the declared conditions.
Uncertainties are determined according to ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 (GUM) and EA-4/02.
Coverage factor k = 2, confidence level about 95 %.
Reference instrument: Fluke 1502A thermometer readout + Pt100 probe (U=0.065 °C, k=2).
Sensor under calibration: DIGITAL thermometer (Measurestream electronics, model 0001-3).

observations_method: Reference temperature T_ref measured by Fluke 1502A + Pt100 (mean per step).
Sensor temperature T_c measured by DIGITAL thermometer (mean per step).
N is the raw sensor reading in LSB (16-bit ADC output, input to the calibration function T = A·D + B).
calculated_calibration_values_method.conclusions: Expanded uncertainty U(E) with coverage factor k = 2, confidence level about 95 %.

calibration_specific_data_method._comment: Fields that are constant across all jobs of this method type. Per-job overrides (IDs, dates, customer) live in job.json.
conditions: Reference: Fluke 1502A readout + Pt100 probe (U=0.065 °C, k=2)
Sensor: DIGITAL thermometer (Measurestream electronics, model 0001-3), resolution 0.01 °C

statements_method: Expanded uncertainty is expressed with coverage factor k = 2 (confidence level about 95%).
notes_lines: Temperatures are expressed in Celsius degrees according to the ITS-90 scale.
Reference temperature T_ref: mean of PT100 (Fluke 1502A) readings at each calibration step.
T_c: reading of the DIGITAL thermometer under calibration at each step.
M_e = T_c - T_ref (signed temperature difference in °C).
Measurement uncertainty U(E) is expanded (k=2), confidence level about 95 %.
pdf_template_data_method.measurement_note_template: Calibration was performed with measurement current {measurement_current} and a {connection_terminals}-terminal connection.
pdf_template_data_method.page4_intro_text: Nella seguente tabella sono riportati i coefficienti dell'equazione lineare di taratura:
pdf_template_data_method.intro_text: This certificate is issued in compliance with the laboratory internal procedure PRO-CAL-MST-003. The results refer exclusively to the identified DIGITAL thermometer and to the declared calibration conditions. After having identified the F. taratura, the calibration function is: T = A · N + B. The calibration was determined over 6 calibration points (0 °C to 125 °C) using a Fluke 1502A metrology well readout and Pt100 reference thermometer.
